2:85.base_text: Then here you are, killing yourselves and evicting a group of your own from their homes—backing each other against them in sin and hostility. And if they come to you as captives, you do ransom them, while their eviction is forbidden to you. Is it that you believe in part of the Scripture and deny part? So what is the repayment for those of you who do that but disgrace in the Earlier Life, and (that) on the Day of Resurrection they are turned back to the most severe punishment? For Allah is never oblivious of what you do.

And wherever you may be, turn your faces towards it. Indeed, those who were brought the Scripture know that it is the truth from their Lord. And Allah is not oblivious of what they do.

2:164.base_text: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the alternation of the night and the day, and the ships that sail the sea with what benefits mankind, and the water that Allah sent down from the sky— with which He revived the earth after it had died and scattered in it all kinds of treading creatures, and the shifting of the winds, and the clouds disposed between the sky and the earth are surely signs for people who reason.

But do not combat them at the Inviolable Mosque unless they combat you about it. So if they combat you, then kill them. Such is the repayment of the deniers.

2:210.base_text: Are they waiting for anything except for (the command of) Allah to come to them in shades of mists, and the angels (too)? And (thus) the matter was settled, for to Allah all things are returned.

2:219.base_text: They ask yousg about wine and gambling. Say, “There is grave sin in them and some benefits for people, but the sin in them is greater than their benefit.” And they ask you about what they should spend. Say, “The surplus.” Thus Allah makes the sign evident to youpl, that you may reflect
2:219.khalaf_hamzah_text: There is much sin in them
2:219.khallad_text: There is much sin in them

2:222.base_text: And they ask yousg about menstruation. Say, “It is (a cause of) harm, so keeppl away from (sexual intercourse with) women during menstruation, and do not approach them until they are clean. But once they have cleansed themselves, then approach them from where Allah has commanded you.” Indeed, Allah loves those who frequently repent, and He loves those who cleanse themselves.”

2:229.base_text: Divorce is (allowed) twice, then either retention in accordance with what is fair, or setting free kindly. And it is not lawful for youpl to take back anything you have given them, unless they both fear that they may not maintain Allah’s limits. So if youpl fear that they may not maintain Allah’s limits, then there is no sin committed by either of them if she pays, as a compensation for her release. These are Allah’s limits, so dopl not transgress them, for those who transgress Allah’s limits are the unjust.
2:229.khalaf_hamzah_text: unless it is feared that they both may not maintain
2:229.khallad_text: unless it is feared that they both may not maintain

2:236.base_text: No sin is committed by youpl if you divorce women before having touched them nor appointed to them any marriage obligation. And compensate them: the wealthy according to his means, and the poor according to his means—a compensation in accordance with what is fair, a duty upon those who excel in doing good.

2:245.base_text: Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan? (So) that He may multiply it for him manifold, for Allah withholds and extends, and to Him you pl are returned.

2:259.base_text: Or like the one who passed by a town and it was completely devastated upon its trellises. He said, “How can Allah revive this after its death?” Thereupon Allah caused him to die for a hundred years and then resurrected him. He said, “For how long have you tarried?” He said, “I have tarried for a day or part of a day.” He said, “Rather, you have tarried for a hundred years. And look at your food and your drink—it has not spoiled—and look at your donkey. And We will make you a sign for mankind. And look at the bones—how We assemble them, then clothe them with flesh.” So when it became evident to him, he said, “I know that Allah is All-Powerful over everything.”

2:271.base_text: If youpl disclose donations, then that is excellent. But if youpl hide them and bring them to the needy, then that is better for you. And He will remit your evil deeds for you. For Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
2:271.khalaf_hamzah_text: then that is better for you, and We will (also) remit your evil deeds for you.
2:271.khallad_text: then that is better for you, and We will (also) remit your evil deeds for you.

2:279.base_text: But if youpl do not, then be informed of a war from Allah and His Messenger. Yet if you repent, then your capitals belong to you— (thus) you do no injustice, and you are not done injustice.

3:154.base_text: Then after the stress He sent down upon youpl security (in the form of) drowsiness overcoming a faction of you, while a faction cared only for themselves, assuming about Allah contrary to the truth—assumptions of the era of ignorance—saying, “Is anything up to us?” Saysg, “Everything is up to Allah.” They hide within themselves what they do not disclose to you. They say, “If it were up to us, none of us would have been killed here.” Saysg, “Even if you had stayed in your houses, those destined to be killed would have emerged to their death beds.” And so that Allah may test what is in your chests and so that He may purge what is in your hearts, for Allah is All-Knowing of what lies within the chests.

3:156.base_text: O you who have attained faith, do not be like those who denied and said of their brethren who traveled in the land or were on an expedition, “Had they stayed with us, they would not have died or got killed.” So that Allah may make it a cause of regret in their hearts. For Allah gives life and causes death, and Allah is All-Seeing of what youpl do.

For Allah gives life and causes death, and Allah is All-Seeing of what they do.

3:161.base_text: And never was it for a prophet to defraud , for whoever defrauds brings whatever he has defrauded on the Day of Resurrection. Then every single self is paid in full for what it has earned, and they are not dealt with unjustly.

3:178.base_text: And never should those who have denied think that We respite them for their own good. In fact, We only respite them so that they may increase in sin, for for them is a demeaning punishment.

3:180.base_text: And never should those who are miserly with what Allah has given them of His grace think that it is good for them. Rather, it is an evil thing for them. They will be encircled by whatever they were miserly with on the Day of Resurrection, for to Allah belongs the inheritance of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is All-Aware of whatever youpl do.

3:181.base_text: Allah has most surely heard the statement of those who said, “Indeed, Allah is poor while we are rich.” We will write down what they said and their unjustified killing of the prophets, and We will say, “Taste the punishment of the burning.

3:195.base_text: So their Lord responded to them: “I do not waste the work of any worker of you, whether male or female—you are of one another. So those who emigrated and were evicted from their homes and were hurt in my way and (who) combated and were killed—I will most surely remit for them their evil deeds and will most surely admit them into Gardens beneath which rivers flow—a recompense from Allah, for with Allah is the excellent recompense.”
3:195.khalaf_hamzah_text: So their Lord responded to them: “I do not waste the work of any worker of you, whether male or female—you are of one another. So those who emigrated and were evicted from their homes and were hurt in my way and were killed and (who) combated—I will most surely remit for them their evil deeds and will most surely admit them into Gardens beneath which rivers flow—a recompense from Allah, for with Allah is the excellent recompense.”

4:1.base_text: O mankind, be mindful of your Lord, Who created you from a single self and created from it its spouse, and from them both spread many men and women. Thus be mindful of Allah—in Whose name you ask one another—and of the wombs. Indeed, Allah has always been Watchful over you.
4:1.khalaf_hamzah_text: O mankind, be mindful of your Lord, Who created you from a single self and created from it its spouse, and from them both spread many men and women. Thus be mindful of Allah, in Whose name, and the name of the wombs, you ask one another. Indeed, Allah has always been Watchful over you.
4:1.khallad_text: O mankind, be mindful of your Lord, Who created you from a single self and created from it its spouse, and from them both spread many men and women. Thus be mindful of Allah, in Whose name, and the name of the wombs, you ask one another. Indeed, Allah has always been Watchful over you.

4:12.base_text: And youpl get one-half of what your spouses have left if they have no children. Yet if they have children, then you get one-fourth of what they have left, after )fulfilling( any bequest they may bequeath and )paying off( any debt. And they get one-fourth of what you have left if you have no children. Yet if you have children, then they get one-eighth of what you have left, after )fulfilling( any bequest you may bequeath and (paying off) any debt. And if a man or a woman is to be inherited by a non-lineal heir while having a brother or sister, then each of them gets one-sixth. But if they are more than that, then they share one-third, after )fulfilling( any bequest that may be bequeathed and )paying off( any debt, without any harm (to anyone). (This is) an injunction from Allah, for Allah is AllKnowing, Clement.
4:12.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . after )fulfilling( any bequest that he may bequeath . . .
4:12.khallad_text: . . . after )fulfilling( any bequest that he may bequeath . . .

4:77.base_text: Have yousg not seen those who were told, “Restrain your hands and establish the prayer and bring the purifying charity”? But when combat was prescribed for them, a group of them feared mankind as only Allah ought to be feared, or even more. And they said, “Our Lord, why did You prescribe combat for us? If only You would delay it for us for a short while.” Saysg, “The enjoyment of the Earlier (Life) is little, while the Hereafter is better for whoever is mindful (of God), and you will not be dealt with unjustly by as much as the thread upon a date-stone.”
4:77.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . and they will not be dealt with unjustly . . .
4:77.khallad_text: . . . and they will not be dealt with unjustly . . .

4:94.base_text: O you who have attained faith, when you travel (to strive) in the way of Allah, then you shall investigate. And do not say to anyone who offers you peace, “You are not a believer,” aspiring for the goods of the Earlier Life, for with Allah is a multitude of gains. You yourselves were like this before, then Allah bestowed favor upon you, so ascertain (the truth). Indeed, Allah has always been All-Aware of what you do.
4:94.khalaf_hamzah_text: And do not say to anyone who offers to surrender to you, “You are not . . .”
4:94.khallad_text: And do not say to anyone who offers to surrender to you, “You are not . . .”

4:114.base_text: There is no good in much of their private conversations, except for one who commands to (even a single) donation or to what is fair or to reconciliation between people. And whoever does that, seeking Allah’s pleasure, then We will bring him a great reward.

4:135.base_text: O you who have attained faith, always stand for justice, as witnesses for the sake of Allah, even if against yourselves or the parents or the close relatives. Whether a person is rich or poor, Allah is worthier as a patron of both. So do not follow prejudice so as to do justice. But if you twist (your testimony) or disregard (giving it) —then indeed Allah has always been All-Aware of what you do.
4:135.khalaf_hamzah_text: But whether you go forward (by testifying) or disregard
4:135.khallad_text: But whether you go forward (by testifying) or disregard

4:140.base_text: For He has surely bestowed upon youpl from on high, in the Scripture, that when you hear Allah’s signs being denied or ridiculed, then do not sit with them until they engage in some other discourse; you would then be like them. Indeed, Allah will gather the hypocrites and the deniers in Hell, all together—
4:140.khalaf_hamzah_text: For it has surely been bestowed upon you
4:140.khallad_text: For it has surely been bestowed upon you

4:152.base_text: But those who have attained faith in Allah and His messengers and have made no distinction between any of them—those, He will bring them their rewards. For Allah has always been Oft-Forgiving, Bestowing of mercy.

4:153.base_text: The People of the Scripture ask yousg to bestow upon them a scripture from heaven. For truly, they had asked Moses for something even greater than that, for they said, “Show us Allah openly.” So the thunderbolt took them on account of their injustice. Then they took the calf for worship after the evident proofs had come to them, yet We pardoned that, and We brought Moses evident authority.

4:162.base_text: But those of them who are firmly rooted in knowledge, and the believers, believe in what was sent down to yousg and in what was sent down before you. And those who establish the prayers, and those who bring the purifying charity, and the believers in Allah and the Last Day—those, We will bring them a great reward.
4:162.khalaf_hamzah_text: He will bring them a great reward.
4:162.khallad_text: He will bring them a great reward.

5:6.base_text: O you who have attained faith, when you rise for prayer, wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, and wipe your heads and (wash) your feet to the ankles. But if you are in a state of major impurity, then cleanse yourselves. But if you are ill or traveling or one of you returns from relieving himself or you have touched women (in intercourse) and cannot find water, then seek clean soil and wipe over your faces and hands with it. Allah does not want to cause you discomfort; rather He wants to cleanse you and to complete His blessing upon you, that you may give thanks.
5:6.khalaf_hamzah_text: wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, and wipe your heads and your feet to the ankles.
5:6.khallad_text: wash your faces and your hands up to the elbows, and wipe your heads and your feet to the ankles.

And you will continue to view treachery on their part except for a few of them, so pardon them and overlook. Indeed, Allah loves those who excel in doing good.
5:13.khalaf_hamzah_text: their hearts fake,” or “. . . their hearts very hard

5:47.base_text: So let the people of the Gospel judge according to what Allah has sent down in it. And whoever does not judge according to what Allah has sent down, then those are the defiantly disobedient.
5:47.khalaf_hamzah_text: And so that the people of the Gospel may judge according . . .
5:47.khallad_text: And so that the people of the Gospel may judge according . . .

5:60.base_text: Say, “Shall I inform you of worse than that as requital from Allah? (It is the retribution of) the one whom Allah has cursed and upon whom is His wrath, and (those) whom He made into apes and pigs, and the worshipers of false masters. Those are in a worse position and further astray from the right way.
5:60.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . and the servants of false masters.
5:60.khallad_text: . . . and the servants of false masters.

5:89.base_text: Allah does not hold youpl accountable for idle talk involving your oaths, but He holds you accountable for your firm binding of oaths; so its atonement is by feeding ten destitute people from the best of what you feed your families, or by clothing them, or by freeing a slave. But if anyone does not find (the means), then fasting for three days. That is the atonement for (breaking) your oaths when you have sworn them. So keep your oaths. Allah thus clarifies His signs for you, that you may give thanks.

5:107.base_text: So if it is discovered that both of them are guilty (of perjury), then two others deserve to take their place from among those responsible for the claim—the two most concerned—and have them swear by Allah, “Our testimony is more truthful than their testimony, and we have not transgressed; we would then most surely be of the unjust.”
5:107.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . from among those who were given the responsibility of the claim—the two . . .
5:107.khallad_text: . . . from among those who were given the responsibility of the claim—the two . . .

5:110.base_text: holy spirit, (making) you speak to people while in the cradle and in the prime of manhood; and when I taught you the Scripture and wisdom and the Torah and the Gospel; and when you create from clay the shape of birds, by My leave, then breathe into it so it becomes birds, by My leave; and when you heal the born-blind and the leper, by My leave; and when you bring out the dead, by My leave; and when I restrained the Children of Israel from you when you brought them the evident proofs. But those who denied among them said, `This is nothing but evident sorcery.’”

5:115.base_text: Allah said, “Indeed, I shall bestow it upon you from on high, but whoever of you denies thereafter, then I shall indeed punish him with a punishment the like of which I never punish anyone of (My creation in) all realms.”

6:16.khalaf_hamzah_text: Whoever He spares on that day—then He has surely had mercy on him
6:16.khallad_text: Whoever He spares on that day—then He has surely had mercy on him

6:23.base_text: Then their only turmoil was when they said, “By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists.”
6:23.khalaf_hamzah_text: By Allah! O our Lord, we were not polytheists.
6:23.khallad_text: By Allah! O our Lord, we were not polytheists.

6:32.base_text: but the Home of the Hereafter is definitely better for those who are mindful (of God). Do you not reason?

6:57.base_text: Say, “I rely on evident proof from my Lord, while you have disbelieved in Him. I do not possess what you ask to be hastened; judgment belongs to none but Allah; He states the truth and He is the Best of judges.”

6:109.base_text: And they swore by Allah their most solemn oaths that if a sign were to come to them, they would believe in it. Say, “Signs are only with Allah,” but how do you know that even if it did come, they would still not believe?

6:114.base_text: “Shall I seek a judge other than Allah when He is the One Who sent down to you the Scripture, explained in detail?” And those to whom We gave the Scripture know that it is the truth, bestowed from on high by your Lord, so never be of those who doubt.

6:119.base_text: And why should you not eat of that over which the Name of Allah has been mentioned when He has detailed for you what He has prohibited for you, except that to which you are compelled? And many do indeed lead (others) astray with their whims without (any) knowledge. Indeed, yoursg Lord knows better who the transgressors are.
6:119.khalaf_hamzah_text: when He has detailed for you what has been prohibited for you
6:119.khallad_text: when He has detailed for you what has been prohibited for you

6:124.base_text: And when a sign comes to them, they say, “We will not believe unless we are brought the like of what was brought to Allah’s messengers.” Allah knows better where to set His message. Abasement (decreed) by Allah will afflict those who committed crimes, and severe punishment (too), on account of what they used to scheme.

6:128.base_text: Thus on the day He herds them all together: “O assembly of unseen beings, you have surely done so much against humans.” But their allies among humans said, “Our Lord, we enjoyed one another, and we have reached the term that you have assigned for us.” He said, “The Fire is your dwelling, wherein you abide, except as Allah wills. Indeed, your Lord is All-Wise, All-Knowing.

6:153.base_text: And (know that) this is My path, straight, so followpl it. And do not follow the (other) ways, lest they divert you from His way. All this He has enjoined upon you, that you may be mindful (of God).
6:153.khalaf_hamzah_text: And indeed, this is my path
6:153.khallad_text: And indeed, this is my path

6:159.base_text: Indeed, the ones who have caused disunity in their religion and became sects—yousg have nothing to do with them. Rather, their case rests with Allah, then He informs them of what they used to do.
6:159.khalaf_hamzah_text: the ones who have departed from their religion
6:159.khallad_text: the ones who have departed from their religion

7:25.base_text: He said, “In it youpl live, and in it you die, and from it you are brought out.”
7:25.khalaf_hamzah_text: and from it you come out.
7:25.khallad_text: and from it you come out.

7:40.base_text: the gates of the heaven will not be widely opened for them
7:40.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . the gates of Heaven will not be opened for them . . .
7:40.khallad_text: . . . the gates of Heaven will not be opened for them . . .

7:44.base_text: Let the curse of Allah be upon the unjust,
7:44.khalaf_hamzah_text: Indeed, the curse of Allah is upon the unjust . . .
7:44.khallad_text: Indeed, the curse of Allah is upon the unjust . . .

7:54.base_text: He causes the night to overwhelm the day, pursuing it persistently, for the sun and the moon and the stars are subjected by His command.
7:54.khalaf_hamzah_text: He causes the night to tightly overwhelm the day . . .
7:54.khallad_text: He causes the night to tightly overwhelm the day . . .

7:57.base_text: And it is He Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings ahead of His mercy until, when they carry up heavy clouds, We drive them to a lifeless land where We send down water, and with it We bring out all kinds of products. Thus We bring out the dead, that you may remind yourselves.
7:57.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . as a revival ahead of His mercy . . .
7:57.khallad_text: . . . as a revival ahead of His mercy . . .

7:81.base_text: “You do approach men with desire instead of women. Rather, you are an extremist people.”
7:81.khalaf_hamzah_text: “Can it be that you do approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are an extremist people.”
7:81.khallad_text: “Can it be that you do approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are an extremist people.”

7:98.base_text: And do the fellows of the towns really feel secure that Our chastisement will not come upon them by day while they are playing?
7:98.khalaf_hamzah_text: Or do the fellows of the towns feel secure that Our chastisement will not . . .
7:98.khallad_text: Or do the fellows of the towns feel secure that Our chastisement will not . . .

7:112.base_text: to bring you every knowledgeable sorcerer
7:112.khalaf_hamzah_text: to bring you every knowledgeable, proficient sorcerer
7:112.khallad_text: to bring you every knowledgeable, proficient sorcerer

7:113.base_text: They said, “Surely there is a reward for us if we are the overcomers.”
7:113.khalaf_hamzah_text: “Is there a reward for us if we are the overcomers?”
7:113.khallad_text: “Is there a reward for us if we are the overcomers?”

7:143.base_text: But when his Lord manifested Himself to the mountain, He turned it into rubble, and Moses fell down unconscious.
7:143.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . He turned it into flattened land . . .
7:143.khallad_text: . . . He turned it into flattened land . . .

7:149.base_text: Then when they felt incapacitated and they realized that they had gone astray, they said, “Unless our Lord extends His mercy to us and forgives us, we will be of the losers.”
7:149.khalaf_hamzah_text: “Unless You extend Your mercy to us, our Lord, and forgive us . . .”
7:149.khallad_text: “Unless You extend Your mercy to us, our Lord, and forgive us . . .”

7:169.base_text: Thus they were succeeded by generations who inherited the Scripture while seizing the materials of this lower (world), and saying, “We will be forgiven.” And should similar materials come their way, they would again seize them. Was not the pledge of the Scripture taken by them, to say nothing about Allah but the truth? Did they not study its contents? But the Home of the Hereafter is better for the mindfulpl (of God). Will you not reason?
7:169.khalaf_hamzah_text: Thus they were succeeded by generations who inherited the Scripture while seizing the materials of this lower (world), and saying, “We will be forgiven.” And should similar materials come their way, they would again seize them. Was not the pledge of the Scripture taken by them, to say nothing about Allah but the truth? Did they not study its contents? But the Home of the Hereafter is better for the mindfulpl (of God). Will they not reason?
7:169.khallad_text: Thus they were succeeded by generations who inherited the Scripture while seizing the materials of this lower (world), and saying, “We will be forgiven.” And should similar materials come their way, they would again seize them. Was not the pledge of the Scripture taken by them, to say nothing about Allah but the truth? Did they not study its contents? But the Home of the Hereafter is better for the mindfulpl (of God). Will they not reason?

8:19.base_text: “If youpl desire a conquest, then the conquest has come to you; and if you desist, it would be better for you. But if you return, We will return; and your battalion, however numerous, will not avail you in anything. And (know) that Allah is with the believers.”
8:19.khalaf_hamzah_text: “If youpl desire a conquest, then the conquest has come to you; and if you desist, it would be better for you. But if you return, We will return; and your battalion, however numerous, will not avail you in anything. For indeed Allah is with the believers.”
8:19.khallad_text: “If youpl desire a conquest, then the conquest has come to you; and if you desist, it would be better for you. But if you return, We will return; and your battalion, however numerous, will not avail you in anything. For indeed Allah is with the believers.”

8:44.base_text: And recall: when youpl met, Him showing them to you as few in your eyes and making you appear fewer in their eyes, so that Allah might carry out a predetermined matter. And to Allah all matters are returned.
8:44.khalaf_hamzah_text: And recall: when youpl met, Him showing them to you as few in your eyes and making you appear fewer in their eyes, so that Allah might carry out a predetermined matter. And to Allah all matters return.
8:44.khallad_text: And recall: when youpl met, Him showing them to you as few in your eyes and making you appear fewer in their eyes, so that Allah might carry out a predetermined matter. And to Allah all matters return.

9:61.base_text: And of them are those who hurt the Prophet and say, “He is just an ear.” Say, “An ear of goodness for you. He has faith in Allah and he has faith in the believers and is a mercy for those of you who have attained faith. And those who hurt the Messenger of Allah—for them is a painful punishment.
9:61.khalaf_hamzah_text: And of them are those who hurt the Prophet and say, “He is just an ear.” Say, “An ear of goodness for you. He has faith in Allah and he has faith in the believers and in mercy for those of you who have attained faith. And those who hurt the Messenger of Allah—for them is a painful punishment.
9:61.khallad_text: And of them are those who hurt the Prophet and say, “He is just an ear.” Say, “An ear of goodness for you. He has faith in Allah and he has faith in the believers and in mercy for those of you who have attained faith. And those who hurt the Messenger of Allah—for them is a painful punishment.

9:66.base_text: Do not make excuses. You have surely denied after having attained faith. If We pardon a faction of you, We will punish another faction because they were criminals.
9:66.khalaf_hamzah_text: Do not make excuses. You have surely denied after having attained faith. If a faction of you is pardoned, We will punish another faction because they were criminals.
9:66.khallad_text: Do not make excuses. You have surely denied after having attained faith. If a faction of you is pardoned, We will punish another faction because they were criminals.

9:111.base_text: Allah has indeed purchased from the believers their (very) selves and their wealth, for which they shall have the Garden (of Paradise, as a reward). They combat in the way of Allah, so they kill and get killed—a promise, binding on Him in the Torah and the Gospel and the Recital. And who is more faithful to his covenant than Allah? So be glad with that trade of yours which you have traded. And that is the great triumph.
9:111.khalaf_hamzah_text: so they get killed and kill
9:111.khallad_text: so they get killed and kill

9:126.base_text: Do they not see that they are tested every year once or twice? Yet they neither repent nor constantly remember.

10:3.base_text: Indeed, your Lord is Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth in six days then settled over the Throne, governing every matter; there is no intercessor except after His permission. Such is Allah, your Lord, so worship Him. Will you not remind yourselves?

10:5.base_text: It is He Who made the sun radiance and the moon a light and determined phases for it, that you may know the number of years and the calculation. Allah did not create all this except with the truth. He details the signs for people who know.

10:18.base_text: And they worship, apart from Allah, what neither harms them nor benefits them, and they say, “These are our intercessors with Allah.” Say, “Are you informing Allah about what He does not know in the heavens or in the earth?” Highly Exalted is He and Most High above whatever they associate (with Him).

10:23.base_text: But when He has saved them, there they are, committing unjustified aggression in the land. O mankind, your oppression is only against yourselves— (you only enjoy) the enjoyment of the Earlier Life, then to Us is your return and We will inform you of what you used to do.
10:23.khalaf_hamzah_text: O mankind, your oppression against yourselves is (only) the enjoyment of the Earlier Life
10:23.khallad_text: O mankind, your oppression against yourselves is (only) the enjoyment of the Earlier Life

10:30.base_text: There and then every single self experiences what it has previously done. And they were returned to Allah, their True Patron, and whatever they used to fabricate strayed away from them.
10:30.khalaf_hamzah_text: every single self reads what it has previously done.
10:30.khallad_text: every single self reads what it has previously done.

10:35.base_text: Say, “Can any of your associates (whom you ascribed to God) guide to the truth?” Say, “Allah guides to the truth. Is the One Who guides to the truth more worthy of being followed or the one who finds no guidance (for himself or for others) unless he is guided? So what is the matter with you? How do you judge?”
10:35.khalaf_hamzah_text: or the one who cannot guide (anyone) unless he is guided?
10:35.khallad_text: or the one who cannot guide (anyone) unless he is guided?

10:45.base_text: And on the day when He herds them, (it will be) as if they had remained for only one hour of a day, getting to know one another. Those who disbelieved the meeting with Allah have certainly lost and were not guided.

10:61.base_text: For yousg do not engage in any affair, nor do yousg read any (portion) of the Recital, nor do youpl do anything, but We are witnessing over youpl as you undertake it. And not even the weight of an atom in the earth or in the heaven escapes yoursg Lord, for nothing smaller than this nor larger but is in an evident record.
10:61.khalaf_hamzah_text: For yousg do not engage in any affair, nor do yousg read any (portion) of the Recital, nor do youpl do anything, but We are witnessing over youpl as you undertake it. And not even the weight of an atom in the earth or in the heaven escapes yoursg Lord, nor is there anything smaller or larger but is in an evident record.
10:61.khallad_text: For yousg do not engage in any affair, nor do yousg read any (portion) of the Recital, nor do youpl do anything, but We are witnessing over youpl as you undertake it. And not even the weight of an atom in the earth or in the heaven escapes yoursg Lord, nor is there anything smaller or larger but is in an evident record.

10:79.base_text: And Pharaoh said, “Bringpl me every knowledgeable sorcerer.”
10:79.khalaf_hamzah_text: And Pharaoh said, “Bringpl me every knowledgeable, proficient sorcerer.”
10:79.khallad_text: And Pharaoh said, “Bringpl me every knowledgeable, proficient sorcerer.”

10:90.base_text: And We made the Children of Israel pass over the sea. So Pharaoh and his t roops pursued them oppressively and aggressively until, when he was about to drown, he said, “I have attained belief that there is no god except the One the Children of Israel believe in, and I am of the Muslims.”
10:90.khalaf_hamzah_text: And We made the Children of Israel pass over the sea. So Pharaoh and his t roops pursued them oppressively and aggressively until, when he was about to drown, he said, “I have attained belief. Indeed, there is no god except the One the Children of Israel believe in, and I am of the Muslims.”
10:90.khallad_text: And We made the Children of Israel pass over the sea. So Pharaoh and his t roops pursued them oppressively and aggressively until, when he was about to drown, he said, “I have attained belief. Indeed, there is no god except the One the Children of Israel believe in, and I am of the Muslims.”

10:103.base_text: Then We deliver Our messengers and those who have attained faith. Thus it is binding upon Us to saved the believers.
10:103.khalaf_hamzah_text: Then We deliver Our messengers and those who have attained faith. Thus it is binding upon Us to deliver the believers.
10:103.khallad_text: Then We deliver Our messengers and those who have attained faith. Thus it is binding upon Us to deliver the believers.

11:7.base_text: And it is He Who created the heavens and the earth in six days —and His Throne was upon the waters—in order to test you as to which of you is better in conduct. And if yousg were to say, “Youpl will be resurrected after death,” those who denied would most surely say, “This is nothing but evident sorcery.”
11:7.khalaf_hamzah_text: And it is He Who created the heavens and the earth in six days —and His Throne was upon the waters—in order to test you as to which of you is better in conduct. And if yousg were to say, “Youpl will be resurrected after death,” those who denied would most surely say, “This is nothing but an evident sorcerer.”
11:7.khallad_text: And it is He Who created the heavens and the earth in six days —and His Throne was upon the waters—in order to test you as to which of you is better in conduct. And if yousg were to say, “Youpl will be resurrected after death,” those who denied would most surely say, “This is nothing but an evident sorcerer.”

11:40.base_text: Until, when Our command came and the oven overflowed, We said, “Carry in it, of each kind, a male and female coupled, and your family—except those against whom the sentence has already been passed—and those who have attained faith.” But none attained faith with him except a few.
11:40.khalaf_hamzah_text: Until, when Our command came and the oven overflowed, We said, “Carry in it, of each female and male kind, one couple, and your family—except those against whom the sentence has already been passed—and those who have attained faith.” But none attained faith with him except a few.
11:40.khallad_text: Until, when Our command came and the oven overflowed, We said, “Carry in it, of each female and male kind, one couple, and your family—except those against whom the sentence has already been passed—and those who have attained faith.” But none attained faith with him except a few.

11:123.base_text: And to Allah belongs the hidden realm of the heavens and the earth, and to Him every matter is returned. So worshipsg Him and place your trust in Him, for your Lord is not oblivious of what youpl do.
11:123.khalaf_hamzah_text: And to Allah belongs the hidden realm of the heavens and the earth, and to Him every matter returns. So worshipsg Him and place your trust in Him, for your Lord is not oblivious of what they do.
11:123.khallad_text: And to Allah belongs the hidden realm of the heavens and the earth, and to Him every matter returns. So worshipsg Him and place your trust in Him, for your Lord is not oblivious of what they do.

12:49.base_text: Then after that comes a year during which people are relieved and during which they press.
12:49.khalaf_hamzah_text: Then after that comes a year during which people are relieved and during which you press.
12:49.khallad_text: Then after that comes a year during which people are relieved and during which you press.

12:63.base_text: So when they returned to their father, they said, “O our father, we were denied measure, so send our brother with us so that we can obtain measure, and we are most surely his preservers.”
12:63.khalaf_hamzah_text: So when they returned to their father, they said, “O our father, we were denied measure, so send our brother with us so that he can obtain measure, and we are most surely his preservers.”
12:63.khallad_text: So when they returned to their father, they said, “O our father, we were denied measure, so send our brother with us so that he can obtain measure, and we are most surely his preservers.”

12:64.base_text: He said, “Am I to entrust him to you as I entrusted his brother to you before? Allah is a Better Preserver, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful ones.”
12:64.khalaf_hamzah_text: He said, “Am I to entrust him to you as I entrusted his brother to you before? Allah is Better in preservation, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful ones.”
12:64.khallad_text: He said, “Am I to entrust him to you as I entrusted his brother to you before? Allah is Better in preservation, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful ones.”

12:109.base_text: And in no way did We send (any messengers) before yousg except men to whom We reveal, from among the people of the towns. Have they not journeyed through the earth and looked at what the outcome was for those before them? The Home of the Hereafter is surely better for those who have been mindful (of God). Do youpl not reason?

12:110.base_text: Until, when the messengers had completely despaired and thought that they had been lied to (by their own selves), Our support came to them. Then whomever We willed was delivered, and Our chastisement is never turned back from the criminal people.
12:110.khalaf_hamzah_text: Then We save whomever We will . . .
12:110.khallad_text: Then We save whomever We will . . .

13:3.base_text: And He is the One Who spread the land and made in it firm mountains and rivers, and of all products he made therein two pairs; He causes the night to overwhelm the day; indeed, in that are signs for people who reflect.
13:3.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . the night to tightly overwhelm the day . . .
13:3.khallad_text: . . . the night to tightly overwhelm the day . . .

13:4.base_text: And in the land are neighboring plots and gardens of vines, and vegetation, and date-palms—from the same root or from distinct roots—irrigated with the same water, yet We make the produce of some taste better than others; most surely, in that are signs for people who reason.

13:39.base_text: Allah erases whatever He decides and He keeps (whatever He decides), and with Him is the source of the Scripture.

14:19.base_text: Have you not seen that Allah created the heavens and the earth with the truth? If He wills, He can do away with youpl and bring a new creation.
14:19.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . that Allah is the Creator of the heavens . . .
14:19.khallad_text: . . . that Allah is the Creator of the heavens . . .

15:59.base_text: except for Lot’s folk; we will most surely deliver them all,
15:59.khalaf_hamzah_text: except for Lot’s folk; we will most surely save them all,
15:59.khallad_text: except for Lot’s folk; we will most surely save them all,

16:1.base_text: Allah’s command has come, so do not rush it. Highly Exalted is He, and Most High above what they associate (with Him).
16:1.khalaf_hamzah_text: Allah’s command has come, so do not rush it. Highly Exalted is He, and Most High above what you associate (with Him).
16:1.khallad_text: Allah’s command has come, so do not rush it. Highly Exalted is He, and Most High above what you associate (with Him).

16:12.base_text: And He has subjected to you the night and the day and the sun and the moon. And the stars are subjected by His command. Indeed, in that are signs for people who reason.
16:12.khalaf_hamzah_text: And He has subjected to you the night and the day and the sun and the moon and the stars—(all) subjected by His command. Indeed, in that are signs for people who reason.
16:12.khallad_text: And He has subjected to you the night and the day and the sun and the moon and the stars—(all) subjected by His command. Indeed, in that are signs for people who reason.

16:20.base_text: Yet those they call upon apart from Allah create nothing, while they (themselves) are created.
16:20.khalaf_hamzah_text: Yet those you call upon apart from Allah create nothing, while they (themselves) are created.
16:20.khallad_text: Yet those you call upon apart from Allah create nothing, while they (themselves) are created.

16:79.base_text: Have they not seen the birds subjected in the midst of the sky? None holds them up except Allah. Surely, in that are signs for people who believe.
16:79.khalaf_hamzah_text: Have youpl not seen the birds subjected in the midst of the sky? None holds them up except Allah. Surely, in that are signs for people who believe.
16:79.khallad_text: Have youpl not seen the birds subjected in the midst of the sky? None holds them up except Allah. Surely, in that are signs for people who believe.

16:96.base_text: What you have runs out, but what is with Allah remains. And We will most surely repay those who were patient their reward in accordance with the best of what they used to do.
16:96.khalaf_hamzah_text: What you have runs out, but what is with Allah remains. And He will most surely repay those who were patient their reward in accordance with the best of what they used to do.
16:96.khallad_text: What you have runs out, but what is with Allah remains. And He will most surely repay those who were patient their reward in accordance with the best of what they used to do.

17:7.base_text: If you excel in doing good, you excel in doing good to your own benefit; and if you do evil, you do so against yourselves. Then when the promise of the second time comes true, they will defile your faces, and they will enter the Mosque as they entered it the first time, and they will utterly destroy all that falls into their power.
17:7.khalaf_hamzah_text: If you excel in doing good, you excel in doing good to your own benefit; and if you do evil, you do so against yourselves. Then when the promise of the second time comes true, He will defile your faces, and they will enter the Mosque as they entered it the first time, and they will utterly destroy all that falls into their power.
17:7.khallad_text: If you excel in doing good, you excel in doing good to your own benefit; and if you do evil, you do so against yourselves. Then when the promise of the second time comes true, He will defile your faces, and they will enter the Mosque as they entered it the first time, and they will utterly destroy all that falls into their power.

17:33.base_text: And do not kill the self which Allah has made inviolable, except in the course of justice. And if someone is killed unjustly, We have given authority to his kinsman who seeks retribution, but he should not be excessive in killing; indeed, he is bound to be aided.
17:33.khalaf_hamzah_text: And do not kill the self which Allah has made inviolable, except in the course of justice. And if someone is killed unjustly, We have given authority to his kinsman who seeks retribution, but yousg should not be excessive in killing; indeed, he is bound to be aided.
17:33.khallad_text: And do not kill the self which Allah has made inviolable, except in the course of justice. And if someone is killed unjustly, We have given authority to his kinsman who seeks retribution, but yousg should not be excessive in killing; indeed, he is bound to be aided.

17:41.base_text: And very truly, We have explained in this Recital in various ways, that they may constantly remember, but it only adds to their aversion.
17:41.khalaf_hamzah_text: And very truly, We have explained in this Recital in various ways, that they may remember, but it only adds to their aversion.
17:41.khallad_text: And very truly, We have explained in this Recital in various ways, that they may remember, but it only adds to their aversion.

17:42.base_text: Say, “If there were other gods with Him as they say, they would have sought a way to the Possessor of the Throne.”
17:42.khalaf_hamzah_text: Say, “If there were other gods with Him as youpl say, they would have sought a way to the Possessor of the Throne.”
17:42.khallad_text: Say, “If there were other gods with Him as youpl say, they would have sought a way to the Possessor of the Throne.”

18:44.base_text: In that situation, protection belongs to Allah, the Truth
18:44.khalaf_hamzah_text: In that situation, authority belongs to Allah, the Truth
18:44.khallad_text: In that situation, authority belongs to Allah, the Truth

18:52.base_text: And on the day when He says
18:52.khalaf_hamzah_text: And on the day when We say
18:52.khallad_text: And on the day when We say

18:59.base_text: and We set an appointment for their moment of annihilation
18:59.khalaf_hamzah_text: and We set an appointment for their annihilation
18:59.khallad_text: and We set an appointment for their annihilation

18:71.base_text: Did you hole it so as to drown its people?
18:71.khalaf_hamzah_text: Did you hole it so that its people would drown?
18:71.khallad_text: Did you hole it so that its people would drown?

18:86.base_text: So he pursued a certain course until, when he had reached the setting of the sun, he found it setting in a murky spring, and found a people in its vicinity.

18:93.base_text: Then he pursued a course until, when he reached the point separating the two barriers, he found beside them a people who could barely comprehend any word.
18:93.khalaf_hamzah_text: . . . who could barely make others comprehend any word.
18:93.khallad_text: . . . who could barely make others comprehend any word.
